DISCOVERY, 302, DEFENDANT BAY CLUB PENINSULA, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION BEYOND SEVEN HOURS WITH DOCUMENT PRODUCTION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF ELENA PHILLIPS IS ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR AN ADDITIONAL 7 HOURS OF DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IN ORDER FOR DEFENDANT TO BE GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY TO FAIRLY DEPOSE HER. (CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 2025.290(A); CERTAINTEED CORPORATION V. SUPERIOR COURT (2014) 222 CALAPP4TH 1053, 1060-1061) DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR DOCUMENT PRODUCTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. A DEPONENT MAY WAIT UNTIL THE DEPOSITION TO RAISE OBJECTIONS. (SEE WEIL & BROWN, CIV. PROC. BEFORE TRIAL (THE RUTTER GROUP 2013) 8:606 [CITING MONARCH HEALTHCARE V. SUPERIOR COURT (2000) 78 CAL.APP.4TH 1282, 1290].) "IF A DEPONENT FAILS TO ANSWER ANY QUESTION OR TO PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENT'UNDER THE DEPONENT'S CONTROL THAT IS SPECIFIED IN THE DEPOSITION NOTICE ', THE PARTY SEEKING DISCOVERY MAY MOVE THE COURT FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING THAT ANSWER OR PRODUCTION." (CODE CIV. PRO., 2025.480(A).) THE MOTION MUST BE MADE WITHIN 60 DAYS OF THE COMPLETION OF THE DEPOSITION RECORD AND MUST CONTAIN A MEET AND CONFER DECLARATION. (CODE CIV. PRO., 2025.480(B).) DEFENDANTS DID NOT SUBMIT A MEET AND CONFER DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION AND THE DEPOSITION HAS NOT BEEN COMPLETED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO SEEKING ADDITIONAL RELIEF IF PLAINTIFF ELENA PHILLIPS EXHIBITS THE SAME EVASIVE AND NONRESPONSIVE BEHAVIOR AT THE SECOND DAY OF HER DEPOSITION. THE SECOND DAY OF DEPOSITION SHALL TAKE PLACE WITHIN 20 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER ON THIS MOTION OR BY AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES. DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER VAN ZANDT, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Anna Dubrovsky of the law offices of Anna Dubrovsky Law Group, Inc. (415-746-1477) appears as counsel for plaintiffs/moving parties

Attorney, Jennifer J. Capabianco of the law offices of Selman Breitman LLP (415-979-0400) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification:

PLAINTIFFs SERGE ELMAN and ELENA PHILLIPS' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS CGC-17-559235 AND CASE CGC-14-542559 is GRANTED and the trial date for the two consolidated cases is now January 14, 2019 at 9:30am in department 206. The two cases involve numerous common questions of law and fact and consolidation furthers judicial economy and avoidance of unnecessary costs. All deadlines for both consolidated cases, including discovery cutoff dates, are trigged off the January 14 trial date. Consolidation is not inconsistent with ruling on defendants' demurrer in case CGC-17-559235 nor is it an impermissible collateral attack on the prior ruling denying Ms. Phillips' request to add claims to this case. Rather, the delay in resolution of this case, for whatever reason, now affords the opportunity to have all claims tried together and the consolidation statute provides the vehicle to do so. Any prejudice to defendants by consolidation has been eliminated by continuing the trial date to November 5, 2018. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes. This case (CGC-14-542559) is the lead case and all further filings must be in the lead case only.

Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.org (L&M302)

Defendant BAY CLUB PENINSULA, LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Appear For Deposition Beyond Seven Hours With Document Production And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiff ELENA PHILLIPS is ORDERED to appear for an additional 7 hours of deposition testimony in order for Defendant to be given the opportunity to fairly depose her. (Code of Civil Procedure 2025.290(a); Certainteed Corporation v. Superior Court (2014) 222 CalApp4th 1053, 1060-1061) Defendant's request for document production is DENIED without prejudice. A deponent may wait until the deposition to raise objections. (See Weil & Brown, Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) 8:606 [citing Monarch Healthcare v. Superior Court (2000) 78 Cal.App.4th 1282, 1290].) "If a deponent fails to answer any question or to produce any document'under the deponent's control that is specified in the deposition notice ', the party seeking discovery may move the court for an order compelling that answer or production." (Code Civ. Pro., 2025.480(a).) The motion must be made within 60 days of the completion of the deposition record and must contain a meet and confer declaration. (Code Civ. Pro., 2025.480(b).) Defendants did not submit a meet and confer declaration in support of the motion and the deposition has not been completed. Defendant's request for sanctions is DENIED without prejudice to seeking additional relief if Plaintiff ELENA PHILLIPS exhibits the same evasive and nonresponsive behavior at the second day of her deposition. The second day of deposition shall take place within 20 days of service of Notice of Entry of the Order on this Motion or by agreement between the parties. Defense counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
